description_long: This object is a decorative trumpet with a unique sculptural design, featuring a detailed figure of a person sitting atop the instrument and interacting with an animal, possibly a dog. The trumpet's bell is wide and flared, with intricate carvings and a smooth transition to the main body. The mouthpiece end is elongated and gently curved, made of a material that contrasts with the lighter, sculpted section. The trumpet is mounted on a round, metallic stand, which supports it upright and adds to its display quality. The surface of the trumpet appears to be a combination of polished wood and carved ivory or bone, giving it a refined, antique appearance. No visible valves or tuning slides are present, emphasizing its ornamental rather than functional nature.
description: A decorative trumpet with a sculpted figure and animal atop its wide, flared bell, mounted on a metallic stand. The instrument features contrasting materials and intricate carvings, giving it an antique, ornamental look.
description_view_0: This view highlights the sculpted figure and animal on top of the trumpet, as well as the wide, flared bell and the smooth, curved mouthpiece.
description_view_1: From the rear, the elongated mouthpiece and the back of the sculpted figure are visible, with the stand supporting the trumpet clearly shown.
description_view_2: This side view emphasizes the length of the trumpet, the detailed carving of the figure, and the smooth transition between the materials.
description_view_3: Looking directly into the bell, the wide opening and the circular shape are prominent, with the figure and animal visible from behind.
attribute_bell_flare: The bell is wide and features a gradual, elegant flare with a sculpted, ornamental rim.
attribute_valve_configuration: No visible valves are present; the trumpet is purely decorative with no functional valve arrangement.
attribute_leadpipe_curvature: The leadpipe is gently curved, extending smoothly from the mouthpiece end toward the bell.
attribute_tuning_slide_location: No main tuning slide is visible; the instrument lacks functional tuning components.
